description_long: This bollard is a robust, cylindrical post resembling a cut section of a tree trunk, complete with naturalistic bark texture and irregular surface features. The exterior is predominantly gray with some brownish undertones, and it displays prominent knobby growths or burls on one side, adding to its organic appearance. The top of the bollard is flat and appears to have a slightly beveled edge, with a smooth, cut surface that contrasts with the rough bark-like sides. The base is square and flat, simulating a concrete or stone mounting plate that sits flush with the ground. There are no visible reflective markings, chains, or connectors, and the object appears solid and heavy, likely intended as a fixed barrier. Its design suggests it is meant to blend into natural or park-like urban environments while serving as an access control barrier.
description: A heavy, tree-trunk-like bollard with a rough, bark-textured cylindrical body, knobby growths, and a flat, square base. The top is flat and smooth, contrasting with the rugged sides.
description_view_0: Front view shows the prominent knobby growths on the left side of the cylindrical post and the flat, square base.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the elongated, rough surface and the protruding burls, with the flat top visible.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals a smoother, less textured side of the bollard with minimal knobby features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the largest cluster of burls and the cylindrical form, with the flat base clearly visible.
